methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Neustark, a Series B climate and energy company, is evaluated against a peer universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage. Key peers include Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), which span a wide valuation range from $120M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using the company's $70M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $133M. The peer-median method, calculated across six same-sector, same-stage comparables, yields a median valuation of $355M, with the peer cohort ranging from $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and market opportunity within climate-tech.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $113M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $244M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty regarding whether Neustark's technology readiness and capital efficiency align with lower-capitalized peers or command multiples closer to sector leadership. Key uncertainties include commercialization timeline, unit economics relative to peers, and the durability of early-stage climate-tech valuations in a shifting investment environment.
